reasoning: The question does not list the resolution dates and appears to have a typo referencing 2025-11-09 as a date in the near future while providing a base value from 2025-10-30. Considering discount window borrowing (primary credit) is highly volatile and depends on idiosyncratic bank liquidity needs, it frequently fluctuates up and down from week to week. In the absence of specific resolution dates or structural indications of broader market stress or easing that would definitively trend this series, the baseline probability for an increase over any given subsequent date is roughly equal to a coin flip, as the series operates near its baseline or moves noisily based on week-to-week bank funding dynamics.
